page: Microsoft Azure FX64-32mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the FXmdsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 16 physical cores and 32 dedicated vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. The system is equipped with 1344.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 42.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 3780 GB of local storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong memory and processing capabilities. The server achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2367.0 (top tier) and a multi-core score of 18564.0 (strong). Memory bandwidth tests show a read-only large block speed of 241.54 GB/sec and a write large block speed of 136.66 GB/sec, both ranking in the top 25%. Single-threaded text processing (2969.0) and image processing (18294.0) on Geekbench also place in the top tier. However, database operations on Passmark (20191.25) and Redis SET operations (50.09 million operations/sec) perform in the average tier.

The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes this server profile suitable for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale caching, and in-memory databases. While it demonstrates strong performance in CPU-bound tasks like bzip3 decompression (205.33 GB/sec multi-threaded) and small-to-medium LLM prompt processing, its lack of a dedicated GPU results in a significant performance tradeoff for large-scale AI workloads, as evidenced by a weak text generation speed of 2.71 tokens/sec on a 70B LLM model. The hardware configuration represents a high-density memory investment, prioritizing massive RAM capacity and local storage over raw GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure FX64-32mds_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine from the FXmdsv2 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8573C processor featuring 16 physical cores, 32 dedicated vCPUs at 4.2 GHz, and a 260 MB L3 cache. The server provides 1344.0 GB of RAM (42.0 GB per core) and 3780 GB of local storage, with no GPU. Benchmarks show top-tier Geekbench single-core (2367.0) and image processing (18294.0) performance, alongside strong memory bandwidth write speeds of 136.66 GB/sec. While it excels in memory-intensive tasks and bzip3 decompression, its lack of hardware acceleration limits large-scale LLM text generation to a weak 2.71 tokens/sec. This configuration is optimized for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and memory-heavy enterprise workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX64-32mds_v2 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 1344 GB RAM, and 3780 GB local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for in-memory databases and data caching.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized server featuring massive RAM capacity, dedicated Intel Xeon processing, and fast local storage for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs at 4.2 GHz.
1344.0 GB of system memory providing 42.0 GB of RAM per core.
3780 GB of local storage with premium storage capabilities.
Top-tier Geekbench single-core and image processing performance.
Strong memory bandwidth and multi-threaded decompression speeds.
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching, and memory-heavy workloads.
